name: John Wu
headline: CS PhD Student at UIUC · AI for Healthcare
bio: Hello! My name is John Wu, a Ph.D. student in the Computer Science Department at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, advised by Professor Jimeng Sun. I am supported by the NSF GRFP.

My research focuses on the transparent deployment of AI systems in healthcare, spanning model interpretability, agentic systems for clinical workflows, and open-source healthcare AI development toolkits. I believe that for AI to be safely adopted in high-stakes clinical settings, it must be both effective and explainable.

I also lead the PyHealth community (400+ members) and run the PyHealth Research Initiative — a program of 10+ researchers actively conducting open-source, reproducible healthcare AI research built on top of PyHealth.

projects.name: PyHealth
projects.description: An open-source Python toolkit for clinical deep learning, unifying 15+ datasets, 20+ clinical tasks, and 25+ models across EHR, signals, and imaging modalities. PyHealth 2.0 features up to 39× faster processing, 20× reduced memory usage, conformal prediction for uncertainty quantification, and multiple interpretability methods. I maintain a 400+ member Discord community around PyHealth and lead the PyHealth Research Initiative — a program of 10+ researchers conducting open-source, reproducible healthcare AI research.

experience.organization: Sun Lab, University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign
highlights: Improved auto-interpretability methods for clinical text classification by over 40% with large language models.
Cleaned and processed clinical text data with NLP toolkits from the de-identified large scale MIMIC dataset.
Increased coverage of conformal prediction of EEG classification by over 10% using distribution shift approaches.
Improved LLM capabilities in low-resource disease detection through quantization and retrieval augmented generation.
Lead maintainer of PyHealth, an open-source toolkit for AI applications in healthcare.
Developed agentic framework to automate clinical predictive modeling with PyHealth.
Designed PyHealth MCP tool framework for AI agent use for automated clinical predictive modeling.
Built frontend UI elements to support clinical predictive modeling within the Data Science Agent product.
Designed agentic framework for price reasoning on Amazon webstore, improving atypical pricing classification performance by 20%.
Constructed agentic tools around product graphs for more intelligent price reasoning.
Revised onboarding technical documents and AWS setup for Bedrock access in EC2 instances.
Published a first-author paper in npj Systems Biology and Applications.
Developed parameter estimation software BioNetGMMFit in C/C++ for mechanistic modeling under noisy conditions.
Improved particle swarm optimization speed by 10× using OpenMP parallel programming.
Accelerated parameter estimation of agent-based models by 1000× through surrogate modeling with deep neural networks in PyTorch.

experience.organization: Rerout Lab, The Ohio State University
